books.title: The Island of Doctor Moreau
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: A shipwrecked man is rescued by a boat and left on a strange island owned by a physiologist who experiments in vivisection.

books.title: The Last of the Mohicans
books.author: James Fenimore Cooper
books.summary: Frontiersmen and Native Americans help transport a colonel’s daughters through upper New York during the French and Indian War.

books.title: You Can’t Win
books.author: Jack Black
books.summary: Jack Black’s autobiographical account of life “on the road” as a professional thief and hobo during the late 19th century and early 20th century.

books.title: A General History of the Pirates
books.author: Captain Charles Johnson
books.summary: The lives and stories of the daring pirate outlaws who terrorized the Atlantic Ocean and Caribbean Sea in the 18th century.

books.title: Emily of New Moon
books.author: L. M. Montgomery
books.summary: A budding poetess and orphan goes to live with her estranged aunts.

books.title: Love Among the Chickens
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: Stanley Ukridge ensnares the narrator in another one of his schemes: running a chicken farm in the English countryside.

books.title: The Way of the World
books.author: William Congreve
books.summary: Two lovers are prevented from marrying due to a previous dalliance.

books.title: The Gods of Pegāna
books.author: Lord Dunsany
books.summary: A mysterious god creates a cosmic pantheon to rule over its worlds and time.

books.title: The Brothers Karamazov
books.author: Fyodor Dostoevsky
books.summary: A dispute over inheritance between father and son escalates into a family feud.

books.title: Frankenstein
books.author: Mary Shelley
books.summary: A tragic scientist creates a monster in his laboratory.

books.title: Incidents in the Life of a Slave Girl
books.author: Linda Brent
books.summary: The autobiography of a woman who grew up in, and escaped from, slavery.

books.title: The Documents in the Case
books.author: Dorothy L. Sayers
books.summary: A man’s apparently accidental death soon arouses suspicions.

books.title: Bulfinch’s Mythology
books.author: Thomas Bulfinch
books.summary: An American classicist collects and explains myths and legends ranging from those of the ancient Greeks and Romans, through stories of King Arthur, to the legends of Charlemagne.

books.title: Tractatus Logico-Philosophicus
books.author: Ludwig Wittgenstein
books.summary: One of the most important texts of 20th century philosophy, in which the nature of the philosophy, language, understanding, and logical atomism are explored.

books.title: New Hampshire
books.author: Robert Frost
books.summary: Robert Frost’s Pulitzer-winning book of poetry.

books.title: The Surprising Adventures of Baron Munchausen
books.author: Rudolph Erich Raspe
books.summary: The legendary Baron Munchausen’s tall tales of wonder and adventure.

books.title: A Strange Manuscript Found in a Copper Cylinder
books.author: James De Mille
books.summary: A shipwrecked sailor travels through a portal to a lost world populated by fantastic creatures and a strange civilization.

books.title: Short Fiction
books.author: Ernest Hemingway
books.summary: A collection of Ernest Hemingway’s short stories and novellas.

books.title: An Inquiry Into the Accordancy of War with the Principles of Christianity
books.author: Jonathan Dymond
books.summary: Dymond argues that war is incompatible with the teachings of Christianity.

books.title: Scrambles Amongst the Alps in the Years 1860–69
books.author: Edward Whymper
books.summary: A classic in early mountaineering literature, describing the first ascent of the Matterhorn and other climbs in the Alps.
